# shopify-deliverable-website-stack-clone


## Key Rules
1. Run tests before declaring work complete
2. Follow existing patterns in the codebase
3. Do not introduce new dependencies without asking

## Anti-Patterns (Do NOT)
- Do not modify configuration files without asking
- Do not reorganize existing directory structure
- Do not add type annotations, docstrings, or comments to code you didn't change

<!-- GUARDRAILS — Generated by LLM-best-practices /bootstrap -->

<investigate_before_answering>
Never speculate about code you have not opened. If the user references a
file, you MUST read it before answering. Do not assume any API, method,
or configuration exists — verify by reading source code or documentation first.
</investigate_before_answering>

<file_creation_control>
Do not create new files unless explicitly asked. Prefer modifying existing
files. Do NOT create: README files for existing projects, wrapper scripts
around existing commands, configuration files the project doesn't need,
separate utility modules when inline code would suffice.
</file_creation_control>

<before_committing>
Before declaring any task complete, verify ALL of the following:
1. All modified files parse/compile without errors
2. All existing tests pass (run the test suite if one exists)
3. No debug code, console.log, or TODO comments left behind
4. Imports reference files that actually exist (no hallucinated imports)
5. Changes match the original task description (re-read it)
6. No unrelated files were modified
</before_committing>

## Available Skills
<!-- Installed by bootstrap — invoke these at any time -->
- `/review-plan` — adversarial plan review before implementation (Product → Architect → Staff → Security → DevOps)
- `/review-implementation` — post-implementation audit vs plan (Coverage → Fidelity → Quality → Integration → DevOps)
- `/signoff` — end-of-session knowledge capture: persists learnings to MEMORY.md and LEARNED RULES
- `/docs-check` — audit Claude Code reference docs for freshness against Anthropic sources
